description: A ground-based rocket launch complex. Required to build and launch spacecraft.
An advanced launch complex with reusable systems and higher throughput. Supports heavy-lift missions.
A permanent crewed station in low orbit. Microgravity research accelerates scientific discovery.
A large multi-module station serving as a construction hub and deep-space research complex.
Combustion plant burning fossil fuels for high-output electricity. Severe environmental cost.
Heavy manufacturing and extraction hub. Boosts material output at significant ecological cost.
Protected natural forest maintained as a carbon sink and ecological buffer.
Utility-scale photovoltaic arrays. Net-positive clean energy with modest environmental benefit.
Grid of wind turbines providing low-overhead renewable energy.
Pressurised habitat modules and ECLSS for an off-world settlement. Consumes consumables; yields light research.
Closed-loop hydroponic food production. Partially offsets colony life-support consumable drain.
Heavy machinery for in-situ resource extraction from planetary regolith.
Orbital research module exploiting microgravity for advanced materials and biology experiments.
Closed-loop life support for station crew. Consumes consumables from the supply chain.
Multi-port docking complex enabling cargo freighter operations. Unlocks transport ship construction.
Planetary-scale atmospheric processors and albedo reflectors. Massive energy draw; accelerates environment recovery by 20 pts/yr.
